task_completion.visual_evidence: The OpenEMR dashboard or main interface is visible, indicating a successful login.

task_completion.description: Locate and select the patient Jayson Fadel.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The patient dashboard, chart, or scheduling interface clearly shows 'Jayson Fadel' (or PID: 3) as the active patient.

task_completion.description: Set the appointment date within the next 14 days and time between 9:00 AM and 12:00 PM.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The scheduling form shows a selected date within 14 days of the current system date, and a start time between 09:00 and 12:00.

task_completion.description: Set the appointment duration to 30 minutes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The scheduling form shows the duration field set to 30 minutes, or the end time is exactly 30 minutes after the start time.

task_completion.description: Enter 'hypertension follow-up' as the appointment comment.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The text 'hypertension follow-up' is visible in the comment, reason, or notes field of the appointment scheduling form.
The appointment appears on the calendar view, or a success message/confirmation screen is displayed showing the scheduled appointment for Jayson Fadel.

integrity.description: The agent used the web browser to interact with the OpenEMR GUI.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show a web browser navigating the OpenEMR interface, not terminal commands, API calls, or database queries.

integrity.description: The agent genuinely navigated the scheduling process.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show intermediate steps like searching for the patient, opening the calendar, and filling out the form, rather than just a final state.

integrity.description: The agent did not spoof the result in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: The outputs are within the actual OpenEMR application, not typed into a text editor, terminal, or mock HTML page.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Patient: Jayson Fadel (PID: 3). Condition SNOMED: 59621000. The appointment must be scheduled within 14 days of the current system date. The time window must be between 09:00 and 12:00. The duration must be 30 minutes. The comment must be exactly 'hypertension follow-up'.
